qualifying_org_definition: Any public or private agency where the ABAWD serves in an unpaid, volunteer capacity (Virginia SNAP Manual Part XV.A.4). No pre-approved site list, MOU, or county assignment required for this lane. SNAP E&T is a separate, voluntary program and not a prerequisite.
verification_instrument: Letter from volunteer supervisor stating number of hours contributed weekly and type of work — confirmed by VaCMS training documentation and first-pass manual review. No named state form exists; the BPS records compliance using the 80-hour determination in VaCMS (field: 'Has this individual worked/volunteered at least 80 hours in a 30 day period'). Letter satisfies Part XV.C.2 documentation requirements. Part XV.C specifies the documentation must include: the number of hours, place, and period of employment/activity.
hours_formula_detail: Part XV.A.4 states volunteers must serve 'at a minimum, for the number of hours that is equal to the household's allotment divided by the federal minimum wage' ($7.25/hr). For a single-person household at max FY2026 allotment ($292), this is ~40 hours/month. The flat 80-hour threshold appears only in Part XV.C for *regaining* eligibility after a gap, not for ongoing compliance. VaCMS training uses the '80 hours in a 30-day period' framing for the regaining-eligibility check, which caused ambiguity in earlier passes.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Virginia SNAP Manual Part XV.A.4 specifies 'any public or private agency' with no geographic restriction on where the organization must be located or registered. No Virginia DSS guidance, transmittal, or policy document reviewed addresses in-state registration requirements for certifying volunteer organizations. Under federal rules (7 CFR 273.24), the lane is open to any qualifying organization; absent an explicit state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide as of July 2025. Virginia SNAP Manual Transmittal 36 (Oct 2025), Part XV Appendix I lists 'No exempt areas' in the July 2025+ column. Prior waivers covering specific localities (e.g., Brunswick, Buchanan, Danville) expired June 2025 and were not renewed, consistent with OBBBA waiver restrictions effective Nov 2025.
submission_deadline: Volunteer hours documentation must be provided within 10 days of a verification request (Part III.A). For ongoing compliance (not regaining eligibility), work hours are verified at each recertification — BPS verifies that volunteering averages 20+ hours/week (per the allotment formula). Changes in work hours must be verified when reported. No named monthly reporting deadline for volunteer hours specifically.
notes:
Virginia SNAP Manual Part XV.A.4 (Transmittal #35, Oct 2024) establishes a general volunteer lane — serving unpaid at any public or private agency with no county assignment or E&T enrollment required. SNAP E&T is explicitly voluntary. No named state verification form was found; verification is by supervisor letter. Confidence is medium because: (1) the manual's hours formula for volunteer work is allotment ÷ federal minimum wage (the workfare formula) rather than a flat 80 hours/month, leaving ambiguity about whether Virginia applies the full federal 80-hour ABAWD standard or only the workfare floor; (2) OBBBA age expansion to 64 (effective Nov 2025) post-dates the Oct 2024 manual; and (3) county administration means local DSS offices may add verification steps not reflected in state policy.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial re-check attempted to refute Tier 1 rating. Four angles searched: (a) work registration as hidden prestep, (b) E&T State Plan FY26 for volunteer gating, (c) approved site list or MOU requirement, (d) legal aid sources for county-level gotchas. No blocking prestep found. Key findings:

1. HOUR STANDARD RESOLVED — 80-hour flat standard confirmed. Virginia's VaCMS training page (SNAP_Work_Requirement.htm) uses the determination 'Has this individual worked/volunteered at least 80 hours in a 30 day period,' which is the federal ABAWD standard, not the allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula. The first-pass ambiguity is resolved.

2. WORK REGISTRATION IS AUTOMATED, NOT A BLOCKING PRESTEP — 'Work registration' in Virginia is an automated status determination applied by the Benefit Programs Specialist (BPS) in VaCMS at application: 'based on responses, VaCMS work registers the client through automation.' The client does not initiate a separate enrollment step; this is an administrative classification, not a gating action. This is not a required_prestep for Tended purposes.

3. SNAP E&T IS VOLUNTARY AND SEPARATE — VaCMS training page asks the BPS 'Does the individual choose to volunteer for SNAPET?' (Yes/No dropdown), confirming enrollment is voluntary. E&T sub-recipients who want to join are given a warm hand-off to the Employment Services Team; those who don't are not blocked from using the general volunteer lane. No required_prestep=EDP or assignment.

4. NO APPROVED SITE LIST OR MOU REQUIREMENT FOUND — Multiple searches for Virginia-specific approved volunteer site list, worksite agreement, or MOU requirement returned no results. The manual language ('any public or private agency') is consistent with an open general lane.

5. VERIFICATION INSTRUMENT CONFIRMED — 'A letter from a volunteer supervisor stating how many hours are contributed weekly and the type of work completed' is confirmed across multiple sources as the verification method. No named state form.

6. OBBBA CONFIRMED ENFORCED — Age range expanded to 18–64, Virginia is enforcing (not waived), effective Dec 1 2025. Manual updated to Transmittal 36 (effective 10/01/2025) which incorporates OBBBA changes (age limit now 65, per Transmittal 36).

7. REMOTE STANCE — No Virginia-specific guidance found prohibiting or explicitly allowing remote/online volunteer hours. Categorized as 'silent' (neither allowed nor restricted by state policy; federal rules do not exclude it).

8. COUNTY VARIATION FLAG MAINTAINED — Fairfax County's SNAPET page conflates general ABAWD volunteer lane with SNAPET enrollment (listing volunteering as a SNAPET component activity), which could cause confusion at the local office level. The state manual is clear, but county DSS offices may apply their own intake practices. Flag remains true.

Conclusion: No prestep, no approved site list, no MOU requirement, no E&T enrollment required, 80-hour flat standard confirmed, supervisor letter is the instrument. All material gaps from first pass are resolved. Tier 1 classification survives adversarial review. Confidence upgraded to HIGH based on: (a) direct VaCMS procedural source resolving the hours-formula ambiguity, (b) multi-source confirmation of no blocking prestep, (c) explicit voluntary E&T framing confirmed in both state plan and VaCMS training, and (d) no evidence of approved-site-list gating after targeted search.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Five new dimensions added via direct PDF extraction of Transmittal 36 (Oct 2025). Key findings per dimension:

1. HOURS FORMULA — Revised to workfare_allotment_over_minwage. Direct text of Part XV.A.4 (both Transmittal 35 and 36) states volunteers must serve 'at a minimum, for the number of hours that is equal to the household's allotment divided by the federal minimum wage.' The flat 80-hour threshold (VaCMS field) applies only to the Part XV.C *regaining eligibility* determination after a benefit gap, not to ongoing volunteer compliance. Representative monthly hours ~40 for a single-person household at max FY2026 allotment ($292 ÷ $7.25).

2. OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT — Silent. Manual says 'any public or private agency' with no geographic restriction. No Virginia transmittal, policy, or guidance document reviewed specifies in-state registration. Out-of-state 501(c)(3)s appear operationally accepted under federal default rules.

3. GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS — None as of July 2025. Transmittal 36, Part XV Appendix I explicitly lists 'No exempt areas' in the July 2025+ column. Prior locality waivers expired June 2025 and were not renewed, consistent with OBBBA restrictions.

4. SUBMISSION UX — No dedicated online portal for volunteer hours submission found. General SNAP changes can be submitted via CommonHelp (online portal) or at the county DSS office. Verification cadence is primarily at recertification per Part III.D; BPS verifies 'volunteering is a weekly average of 20 hours or more.' Recipients have 10 days to provide documentation when requested. No named monthly reporting deadline for volunteer hours.

5. FORM FIELDS — Part XV.C specifies documentation must include 'number of hours, place, and period of employment.' Earlier source confirmed letter must state 'how many hours are contributed weekly and the type of work completed.' Combined required fields: org_name, org_signature, supervisor_name, recipient_name, hours_total, hours_by_day_or_week (weekly average), activity_description, period_start_date, period_end_date. No notarization or EIN requirement found.
